description:
Extend `nebula.types.host` and the feature system to model devboxes properly.

## Changes

### 1. New host options (`modules/nebula/types.nix`)
Add to the `host` type:
- `devbox.hypervisor` — enum: `"utm"` | `"kvm"` | `"vmware"` | `null` (default null, meaning not a devbox)
- `devbox.guest` — enum: `"linux"` | `"macos"` (default `"linux"`)
- `devbox.parentHost` — string, optional — the host key of the machine that runs this VM (e.g., `"ash"` for `ash-devbox`)

### 2. New feature flags (`modules/hosts.nix`)
Add to `nebula.features`:
- `"devbox"` — replaces `"fusion"` as the general "this is a VM" indicator
- `"utm"` — UTM hypervisor
- `"kvm"` — KVM hypervisor

Keep `"fusion"` for backward compat during migration.

### 3. Feature derivation (`modules/nebula/helpers.nix`)
Auto-derive features from devbox config:
- `devbox.hypervisor = "utm"` → features include `"devbox"` and `"utm"`
- `devbox.hypervisor = "kvm"` → features include `"devbox"` and `"kvm"`
- `devbox.hypervisor = "vmware"` → features include `"devbox"` and `"fusion"`

### 4. Update existing devbox hosts
Migrate `ash-devbox` and `spectre-devbox` to use the new schema:
```nix
ash-devbox = {
  # ... existing config ...
  devbox = {
    hypervisor = "utm";
    guest = "linux";
    parentHost = "ash";
  };
  features = [ "managed" "nixos" "work" "tailscale" ];
  # "fusion" removed, "devbox"/"utm" auto-derived
};
```

## Acceptance
- `nix eval .#nebula.hosts.ash-devbox.devbox.hypervisor` returns `"utm"`
- Feature system correctly derives `devbox` and `utm` features
- Existing configurations still build (backward compat with fusion)
title: Extend host type with devbox/hypervisor options
